questionHtml:
What are linked by hydrogen bonds?

A. Hydrogen and oxygen within a water molecule

B. Phosphate and sugar within a DNA molecule

C. Base and sugar between DNA nucleotides

D. Hydrogen and oxygen in different water molecules

meta.title: What are linked by hydrogen bonds? A. Hydrogen and oxygen within a water molecule B. Phosphate and sugar within a DNA molecule C. Base and sugar between DNA nucleotides D. Hydrogen
breadcrumbLabels: A1.2.6. DNA as a double helix made of two antiparallel strands of nucleotides with two strands linked by hydrogen bonding between complementary base pairs
A1.1.2. Hydrogen bonds as a consequence of the polar covalent bonds within water molecules
